reasoning: I voted for Modi in 2019 and I'm voting for him again. I spent two years in Gujarat and I saw how proper governance actually works—roads that work, systems that work, no constant drama. I came back here and it's the same chaos every day: TMC gundas like Shahjahan grabbing land, running fiefs like kingdoms, stealing from schemes meant for poor people. My father was right all along. Biswajit Das keeps flipping between parties like a leaf in the wind—no principles, just following power. Shantanu Thakur at least delivered on the CAA certificates for the Matuas, even if it took four years. That's what I'm talking about—BJP actually does things. TMC would have kept that hanging forever. I need a government that works, not one that just steals and argues with itself.
primary_drivers: Modi's governance model from Gujarat experience—roads, systems, competence
Anti-TMC corruption narrative—Sandeshkhali, scheme theft, muscle politics
Belief that BJP delivers on promises while TMC just manages vote banks
